text: we should take into consideration an advice of our parents too
comments: I coded this as decision-making because girls explain their rating in terms of listening to parents. It is nuanced because advice is considered, but not all decisions are made by parents.

text: some of decisions are making ourselves but not all of them
comments: This is a strong nuanced agency statement: girls make some decisions, but not all.

text: even for small children itís a big pride
comments: Education is linked to dignity and status, so broad aspiration.

text: we need education
comments: Direct statement valuing education.

text: we need to go to school for study, for having many friends
comments: This reflects positive aspiration for schooling including learning and social life.

text: Basically boys and girls in our district study until finishing college, itís compulsory education in our country
comments: This suggests no major gender difference through college/vocational completion.

text: then some of us want to continue
comments: Indicates some continue further; no clear gendered gap here.

text: but some donít want to study in the institutes
comments: This may reflect a smaller gap because continuation is not universal, but the reason is not fully structural.

text: most of us dream and trying to get high education
comments: This is a direct higher-education aspiration.

text: we understand that prosperous future is not possible without high education
comments: Broad aspiration for a better future through education.

text: most of our parents understand the importance of education
comments: This supports a generally positive norm around educating girls.

text: but there is another part of parents who think that girls donít need education so much as boys
comments: Direct evidence of unequal valuation of girlsí education.

text: we know how plant wheat and vegetables, cotton and fruits and the process of their growing
comments: Direct statement of girlsí agricultural knowledge.

text: most important that we like agricultural works because we are living in rural areas
comments: This expresses a positive agricultural livelihood orientation.

text: we like to plant beautiful flowers
comments: This is a lighter aspirational statement around cultivation.

text: Financial issues
comments: Directly named as a major reason girls end education.

text: Parents donít give permission to live in big cities
comments: This is a mobility restriction affecting girlsí educational/work prospects.

text: Early Marriage
comments: Early marriage disrupts girlsí attainment.

text: Girls donít want to study themselves
comments: This is a less structural explanation, so I coded it as a smaller gap.

text: Early engagement in market business
comments: Early work engagement interrupts schooling.

text: Parents donít give permission to live in big cities
comments: Explicitly ranked as a top reason, showing mobility/family control barrier.

text: I would like to be a designer
comments: Clear occupational aspiration.

text: I want to be both a doctor and business lady because I want to open my own stomatology center
comments: This combines professional and business aspiration; I coded the occupation part here.

text: I want to open my own stomatology center
comments: Clear business/enterprise aspiration.

text: I want to organize greenhouse and plant all vegetables during a year
comments: This is an enterprise-oriented agricultural aspiration.

text: I want to be a farmer and raise very good wheat for all our people in district
comments: Clear aspiration in agriculture.

text: Mostly they are busy in our local market
comments: This describes common post-study livelihoods for young women.

text: Another way ñ they help their parents and continue to work on farms or new private mills
comments: This shows continued family labor obligations alongside work.

text: some girls are working in private bakery
comments: Concrete occupational pathway.

text: she can support her husbandís business or open her own farm
comments: Business/enterprise aspiration in farming.

text: she can open own bakery
comments: Direct business aspiration.

text: she can open small family business or sewing business
comments: Direct business aspiration.

text: Development of local markets, new shops
comments: Recognized as important economic innovation.

text: Development of farms
comments: Recognized as important innovation.

text: Development of greenhouses
comments: Recognized as important innovation for girls.

text: New kind of seeds from Holland, Turkey
comments: Recognized as important agricultural innovation.

text: New tractors and combines
comments: Recognized as important innovation, especially for boys.

text: Selling of milk production
comments: Recognized as important female-associated innovation/practice.

text: The most important for girls is development of greenhouses and development of farms
comments: These are seen as important productive opportunities for girls.

text: young girls could be married and work in the husbandís house in greenhouses and have their incomes
comments: This links girlsí future livelihoods to greenhouse work and earning income.

text: The most important for boys is development of markets and appearance of new modern tractors and combines
comments: This reflects boysí opportunities in technical and market-oriented work.

text: We think that these points more important for boys because they could find a lot of jobs that they like technical equipment and could drive it
comments: Directly associates technical equipment and driving with boys.

text: Yes boys and girls could apply to the same organizations because we have equal access
comments: This directly supports equal formal opportunity for boys and girls.

text: but our opportunities are different
comments: This complicates the previous statement, so I coded it as nuanced.

text: parents more checking on girls than boys and require to be the girls to be closer to their house
comments: Clear mobility and supervision restriction affecting girls.

text: But parentsí relationships to the girls are quite different after marriage
comments: Suggests mobility and control norms may shift after marriage, so nuanced.

text: a lot of new kinds of seeds appeared in our district
comments: Direct explanation of agricultural change through seed innovation.

text: Wheat and rice are planted more and more in the last 10 years
comments: Indicates changing cropping patterns over time.

text: Some of them follow to this traditions
comments: Indicates some continuity of gendered agricultural traditions.

text: we think that young girls are working on more unpaid or less paid work compared to boys
comments: This clearly indicates unequal and gendered labor valuation.

text: usually after marriage young boys become more following to traditions and leave for women more routine work as before
comments: Shows marriage strengthens traditional labor divisions.

text: all preparation works on the fields are doing by girls and women
comments: Clear gendered assignment of routine agricultural work to women.

text: they will not face with difficulties
comments: Opposes strong restrictions on boys/men or opposite-sex members taking on that crop activity.

text: In some cases girls could face with some difficulties because of their age
comments: This suggests young girlsí public selling is constrained by age and social norms.

text: itís not good for young girls to work at market and sell her products
comments: Direct normative stigma against young girls selling in markets.

text: she can raise plants but boys should sell on the market
comments: Clear division: girls produce, boys sell.

text: no, all family members are responsible for the same plot, they could not have different plots
comments: Opposes separate gendered plots.

text: Man is working in home garden very seldom
comments: Indicates some differentiated pattern of labor by space and task.

text: women are working on farm plot almost every day
comments: Clear statement of womenís heavy labor burden.

text: Some children more helping their mother than his father
comments: Suggests support around womenís tasks, but still somewhat gendered.

text: and on farm plots they could help her too
comments: Indicates some assistance on womenís work/plots.

text: but husbandís relatives usually donít help her
comments: Suggests womenís plots/work receive less wider-family support.

text: each woman will ask from her husband, itís one of the ways of diplomacy
comments: Strongly indicates women must consult husbands, framed as required diplomacy.

text: she will ask from specialists too
comments: Women do have access to specialists as information sources.

text: she could apply to professional organizations too
comments: Indicates formal organizational access for women.

text: Man will ask from other specialists but not from women
comments: Womenís knowledge is explicitly excluded from menís consultation networks.

text: yes, this priority direction of consulting with the spouse is for women only
comments: This confirms a gender-asymmetric expectation that women must consult husbands.

text: he will not give permission to Gulya
comments: Direct restriction on joining a local womenís group.

text: he will not give permission to Gulya
comments: Direct restriction on selling vegetables herself.

text: no, he ask for more help but not less
comments: Confirms womenís labor burden remains even when they are busier.

text: people will respect her, itís usual thing for our community
comments: Opposes stigma against market-active women.

text: Some men will laugh at him; because of his taking care of his children
comments: Direct stigma against men doing childcare.

text: soon they will invite him to ìChayhonaî (teahouse)
comments: This is a softer social reaction; some teasing/social correction rather than severe exclusion.

text: he will be very glad
comments: Positive reaction to wifeís enterprise success.

text: gender equality is a good thing
comments: Direct positive assessment of gender equality.

text: but it seems to me that itís uncomfortable for man
comments: Equality is seen as positive but potentially challenging for men, so nuanced.

text: but we need gender equality for societyís development
comments: Clear endorsement of equality as socially beneficial.

text: they will decide everything jointly, in cooperation with each other
comments: Strong joint decision-making statement.

text: they will discuss and decide jointly, who has more time will sell their products
comments: Opposes fixed gender role in selling by making it time-based.

text: Sure, because this spouse more thinking about effectiveness of the innovation
comments: Positive statement about adopting effective innovation.

text: She will explain him everything and he will understand
comments: Suggests some ability for woman to proceed, but through persuasion, so nuanced asset control.

text: for him (for men) it is easy to get agreement from his wife
comments: Indicates male advantage in moving forward with inherited money.

text: A wife's agreement is nowhere required for men
comments: Very clear asymmetry in menís control over assets and decisions.

text: yes, in our district she can
comments: Opposes restriction on women walking to the market alone.

text: young married woman easily walk around
comments: Opposes restriction on married womenís local movement.

text: itís easy for women to sell products on the market. It is a usual thing today
comments: Strong opposition to the idea that selling is hard for women.

text: no, girls are not migrating
comments: Clear restriction on girlsí migration for work.

text: These types of girls are not respected in our community
comments: Strong stigma against girls who migrate and return.

text: we think that for girls ñ 19-23 years old and for boys 23 ñ 26 years old
comments: Different marriage ages indicate gendered expectations.

text: there are two types of marriage
comments: Introduces some variety in how marriage decisions happen.

text: first ñ agreement of parents
comments: Direct parental control route in marriage decisions.

text: second ñ joint agreement of young people
comments: Shows that some marriages are based on youth choice.

text: mostly boys have more chances to choose a girl
comments: Indicates unequal but not absent female agency in spouse choice.

text: girls more waiting to be chosen by a good boy
comments: Strongly reflects womenís more passive role in marriage choice.

text: everybody dreams about a Prince at our age and we are dreaming too
comments: This is a relationship/marriage aspiration rather than occupational one.

text: we think it should be joint decision of young people and parents
comments: Supports shared decision-making in marriage.

text: yes, we agree because our parents have rich life experience
comments: This supports joint decision but still gives weight to elder authority, so nuanced.

text: women typically wait for a first child from 20 ñ 24 years
comments: Normative timing statement, but not strongly equality-related; lower confidence.

text: we think its good age
comments: Positive evaluation of current age at first child.

text: at present time we have no cases of early marriage
comments: Positive sign of delayed marriage.

text: usually girls married after finishing of college
comments: Supports education before marriage.

text: from 21-22 years girls could give a birth to a child
comments: Preferred later age for first child.

text: we think that at this age itís not badly influence on our reproductive health
comments: Justifies later childbirth in health terms.

text: Because besides birth she will take care for that child for a long time
comments: Justifies later childbirth in caregiving terms.

text: we think that spouses are planning the date and number of childís themselves
comments: Strong support for couple-controlled fertility decisions.

text: however, in some cases mother-in-law could also influence on these decisions
comments: Family influence remains in some cases, so nuanced.

text: young families could apply to doctors and advice with them too
comments: Indicates access to professional advice around fertility decisions.

text: we would like to have changes in the field of utilities service; have always power house, in school, access to potable water, gas for our houses heating
comments: Broad aspiration for better living infrastructure.

text: we would like to have new Centers for youth with exceptional abilities
comments: Aspiration for youth development spaces.

text: with many modern courses for creative development
comments: Aspiration for skill and creative development opportunities.

text: To open new factories and create conditions for their development and employment
comments: Broad aspiration for local development and jobs, especially for boys.

text: To help young boys to find here new jobs and donít go for working abroad
comments: This implies lack of local jobs currently and the need to reduce work migration.

text: May be some new agricultural Modern Centers for Youth
comments: Aspiration for modern agriculture-oriented youth development.
